design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore locations, talk to NPCs for information and items, solve inventory-based puzzles using correct item-command combinations to progress through a linear story
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Adventure/puzzle-solving tension rather than action tension; player must discover correct puzzle solutions through dialogue and experimentation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a wizard and rescue a mentor from an evil sorcerer in a magical parallel world; experience a comedic fantasy adventure with literary parody
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy commitment to comedy and literary parody (Discworld, Lord of the Rings, fairy tales) as primary narrative vehicle; adventure game that prioritizes humor and writing quality over mechanical challenge; employs puzzle-gating rather than action-gating for progression

difficulty.checkpointing: Manual save/load via in-game postcard system; no death penalty described
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Non-punitive puzzle adventure; player can explore at own pace, save at will, and retry puzzle solutions without consequence

progression.upgrade_system: Progression through becoming a wizard (achieved via task completion and fee payment to guild of wizards); acquisition of specific items needed to breach Sordid's mountain lair
progression.unlock_mechanism: NPC dialogue gates progression; must talk to specific characters to receive tasks, items, and information; tasks unlock wizard status and subsequent areas
replayability_hooks: Comedy writing and character interactions encourage replay for dialogue
Fantasy parody elements reward familiarity with source material on replay

world_building_techniques: Environmental parody (locations parody fairy tales and fantasy novels)
NPC dialogue as primary narrative delivery (talking to people for information is core gameplay)
Inventory-based story progression (obtaining specific items unlocks plot beats)
Setting juxtaposition (magical world contains modern elements, creating tonal comedy)
Character design referencing (protagonist inspired by Blackadder; Wise Owl modeled on Patrick Moore)

boss_design.philosophy: Final antagonist Sordid is puzzle-based rather than action-based; defeated through environmental interaction (pushing into fiery pits) after obtaining necessary items and potion to shrink and explore his tower
notable_bosses: Sordid (evil sorcerer final antagonist; defeated via environmental puzzle rather than combat)

faction_indicators: Guild of wizards (village pub-based; establishes wizard status through formal task/fee system)
Sordid's followers/minions (goblins encountered early; implied power structure under evil sorcerer)
danger_signals: Evil sorcerer Sordid kidnapped the mentor wizard Calypso
Sordid created a wand capable of turning people to stone
Sordid's mountain lair is heavily fortified (requires specific items/potions to breach)
environmental_storytelling: Parallel universe mixing magical and modern elements (establishes world tone)
Wizard tower interiors with specific rooms indicating power and purpose
The Fiery Pits of Rondor as final dungeon location (Sordid's intended destruction site)
Calypso's home as safe haven and quest origin point
